content.problemStatement:
There is an array of NNN integers, and a range [L,R][L, R][L,R].

Let f(A)=∏1≤i<j≤N(Ai⊕Aj)f(A) = \prod_{1 \le i < j \le N} (A_i \oplus A_j)f(A)=∏1≤i<j≤N​(Ai​⊕Aj​). Informally, f(A)f(A)f(A) is the product of all pairwise XOR's of the array AAA.

If the resulting value lies between LLL and RRR (both inclusive), we call such an array a **Halloween array**.

Given an array, determine whether it is a **Halloween array** or not. Print YES\text{YES}YES or NO\text{NO}NO accordingly.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of input contains NNN, LLL and RRR - the size of the array, and the left and right bounds of the interval respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.




Output Format
Output on a new line YES\text{YES}YES if the array is a halloween array else NO\text{NO}NO.


It is allowed to output each character in either case. yes\text{yes}yes, YeS\text{YeS}YeS will also be considered as a positive response.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : f(A)=(1⊕3)=2f(A) = (1 \oplus 3) = 2f(A)=(1⊕3)=2. 222 lies between 000 and 101010, hence it is a Halloween array.
Test Case 2 : f(A)=2f(A) = 2f(A)=2. 222 does not lie between 333 and 444.
